name: node-tar <7.5.16 — 7 CVEs (path traversal, symlink poisoning) [devDep]
description: tar@7.5.2 (transitive via @expo/cli in root package.json) has path-traversal, symlink poisoning, and TOCTOU race CVEs fixed in >=7.5.16. pnpm-lock.yaml line 12367 confirms tar@7.5.2 resolves, and line 15152 shows @expo/cli uses it. The vulnerability is exploitable when extracting attacker-controlled tarballs — @expo/cli extracts packages/archives during `expo install` and dependency management. This is a devDep chain. Fix: add pnpm.overrides "tar": ">=7.5.16" in root package.json and re-lock.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: tar@7.5.2 pinned transitively via @expo/cli (pnpm-lock.yaml:12367 and :15152); path-traversal/symlink-poisoning/TOCTOU-race CVEs fixed >=7.5.16. Variants: (a) path traversal via malicious tarball entry with "../" sequences during `expo install` package extraction; (b) symlink poisoning where a tarball entry creates a symlink pointing outside the target dir; (c) TOCTOU race between stat and extract allowing write to unintended path — three distinct exploit surfaces, all triggered only when extracting attacker-controlled tarballs in @expo/cli devDep context. Fix: pnpm.overrides "tar": ">=7.5.16" in root package.json. Verification: `pnpm install` succeeds; `pnpm-lock.yaml` no longer resolves tar@7.5.2; `pnpm audit` no longer flags tar advisories; `pnpm exec nx run mobile:typecheck` passes. Scanner source: package.json (root) → @expo/cli → tar@<7.5.16 (FCR 2026-06-21 L13-14). Regression test not feasible as unit test (OS-level tarball extraction); audit-clean + lockfile upgrade to >=7.5.16 + typecheck green is the verification gate.
riskImpact: P1: path traversal and symlink poisoning during tarball extraction via @expo/cli; exploitable by attacker-controlled packages during `expo install` on developer machines or CI; devDep-only but write-to-filesystem impact.
